title: 10 best herbal capsules for dandruff

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesame capsules helps with dandruff because they contain natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that effectively combat fungal infections causing flaking and itching. The capsules' sesamin content also reduces inflammation, soothing scalp irritation and promoting a healthy environment for hair growth. Additionally, sesame oil's moisturizing effects help to lock in moisture, reducing dryness and flakes associated with dandruff. Regular use of herbal sesame capsules can lead to a significant reduction in dandruff symptoms, leaving the scalp feeling calm, clear, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antifungal property: Sesame oil contains antifungal compounds that help to prevent the growth of fungus on the scalp, which can contribute to dandruff and other scalp issues.

Anti-inflammatory property: Sesame oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with dandruff, making it a soothing and calming treatment for irritated scalps.
Emollient property: Sesame oil is rich in emollients that moisturize the scalp, reducing flaking and scaling caused by dryness and irritation, and helping to promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_constituents: Phenolics: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the inflammation and irritation associated with dandruff, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Fatty acids: Sesamum indicum is rich in fatty acids, such as oleic and linoleic acid, which can help moisturize and soothe the scalp, reducing flakiness and itchiness associated with dandruff.
Triterpenoids: These compounds have been shown to have antifungal properties, which can help control the growth of fungi that contribute to dandruff, promoting a balanced scalp microbiome.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Sesame oil extracted from the seeds is used in capsules for dandruff treatment due to its anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Sesamum indicum are used in capsules for their antioxidant and soothing effects, which help in calming the scalp and reducing dandruff.
Seeds: Sesame seeds are also used in capsules for their antifungal properties, which help in controlling fungal infections that can cause dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of sesamum indicum seeds and dry them for 7 days to achieve optimal potency.
Grind the dried seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the sesamum indicum powder with 20 grams of gelatin and 10 grams of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill a capsule machine with the gelatin mixture and produce 1000 capsules in 10 minutes.
Label and store the sesamum indicum capsules in an airtight container for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree capsules helps with dandruff because of its potent antifungal and antibacterial properties. The active ingredient, tea tree oil, effectively targets and eliminates the underlying causes of dandruff, including fungus and bacteria that contribute to flaking and itching. As the capsules are absorbed into the body, they work from within to reduce inflammation and soothe the scalp, leading to a noticeable reduction in flakes and irritation, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment.
remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil: The active ingredient in tea tree oil, melaleuca alternifolia, has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to reduce the growth of yeast-like fungus Malassezia, which contributes to dandruff.
Antioxidants: Herbal tea tree capsules contain antioxidants like polyphenols and flavonoids that help to soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing inflammation and redness associated with dandruff.

Anti-inflammatory Compounds: Certain compounds present in the herbal extract, such as terpenes and phenolic acids, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce itching and scaling caused by dandruff.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Helps with dandruff by its ability to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of fungi that cause scalp infections.
Linalool: Assists in treating dandruff by its antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which help to control the scalp's fungal and bacterial population.
Terpinen-4-ol: Contributes to the treatment of dandruff by its ability to penetrate the scalp's hair follicles and roots, reducing fungal and bacterial growth, and soothing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the primary source for Tea Tree oil, which is widely used in shampoos and capsules to treat dandruff due to its antifungal and antibacterial properties.
Seeds: Some products may contain Tea Tree seed extract, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the scalp.
Barks: Although less commonly used, some products may contain Tea Tree bark extract, which is thought to have antioxidant properties that help protect the scalp from damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried leaves and flowers from melaleuca alternifolia plants with dark green color.
Dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a high-speed blender for 5 minutes.
Fill 500 empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the melaleuca alternifolia powder each.
Seal the filled capsules using a gelatin capsule sealer at a pressure of 10 tons per square inch.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with dandruff because of their ability to soothe and calm the scalp. The aloe vera present in the capsules penetrates deep into the skin, reducing inflammation and irritation that can cause flakes to form. Additionally, aloe has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to combat the underlying infections that contribute to dandruff. By addressing these underlying causes, aloe capsules can effectively reduce symptoms of dandruff, leaving the scalp healthy, flake-free, and itch-free.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with dandruff.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: The antimicrobial compounds in aloe vera capsules help eliminate fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to dandruff, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Soothing and moisturizing properties: Aloe vera's gel-like substance provides hydration and soothes irritated skin, reducing flaking and itching caused by dryness or irritation.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is an anthraquinone compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, helping to soothe and calm the scalp to reduce dandruff.
Lupeol: Lupeol is a triterpene compound that possesses anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to control the growth of yeast and other microorganisms that contribute to dandruff.

Bis-benzylisoquinoline alkaloid: This compound has been shown to have antifungal and antibacterial properties, helping to inhibit the growth of pathogens that can cause dandruff and other scalp disorders.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most commonly used part of Aloe vera for treating dandruff, due to their high content of anti-inflammatory and antifungal compounds.
Gel from Leaves: The gel extracted from the leaves is rich in vitamins, minerals, and other nutrients that help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing dandruff and flaking.

Aloe Vera Juice from Leaves: The juice extracted from the leaves contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation and irritation on the scalp, thereby treating dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves and cut them into small pieces, using about 200g of the leaves.
Dry the aloe vera pieces in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried aloe vera into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix 1:1 ratio of aloe vera powder with gelatin or cellulose powder and add 10ml of distilled water.
Fill the mixture into empty vegetarian capsules, 500mg per capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with dandruff because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric capsules reduces inflammation in the scalp, which is a primary cause of dandruff. Additionally, curcumin's antifungal properties help combat Malassezia, a yeast that contributes to dandruff. By reducing inflammation and combating fungal growth, turmeric capsules provide relief from flaky skin, itching, and redness associated with dandruff, promoting a healthy scalp and manageable flakes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcuminoids, can help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which is often a underlying cause of dandruff, and alleviate symptoms like itching and redness.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: The bioactive compounds in turmeric, including curcuminoids and essential oils, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria that can contribute to dandruff, helping to prevent infection and promote a healthy scalp environment.

Antioxidant property: Turmeric's antioxidants can help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress that exacerbate dandruff symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: This polyphenolic compound exhibits potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with dandruff, promoting a healthy scalp.

Volatiles (Beta-Caryophyllene, Limonene, and Pinene): These terpenes have antimicrobial properties, which help inhibit the growth of Malassezia yeast, a common cause of dandruff, thereby reducing flaking and itching.
Demethoxycurcumin: This compound has been shown to exhibit antifungal properties, specifically against Malassezia, which helps control the yeast's overgrowth and alleviate dandruff symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce dandruff.
Roots: They are used because they contain a high amount of curcumin and other active compounds that help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing dandruff symptoms.
Leaves: They are used as they contain essential oils and other compounds that have antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to prevent the growth of fungi that cause dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 grams of dried curcuma longa rhizomes and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the powdered curcuma longa with 250 grams of gelatin and 50 grams of water in a large bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the gelatin dissolves completely in 5-7 minutes.
Remove the bowl from heat and let it cool for 10-15 minutes before pouring it into a capsule mold.
Allow the mixture to set in the mold for 30-40 minutes before popping out the herbal capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with dandruff because they contain antioxidants, anti-inflammatory properties, and natural antifungals that work synergistically to soothe and calm the scalp. The bitter compounds in dandelion root also help to regulate oil production, reducing flaking and itchiness caused by an overactive sebaceous gland. Additionally, dandelion's antiseptic properties help to combat fungal infections that can contribute to dandruff, promoting a healthy scalp and hair growth.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidant compounds help combat free radicals that can damage hair follicles, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy scalp function.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion capsules, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, can reduce redness, itching, and irritation associated with dandruff, promoting a healthier scalp environment.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Dandelion's antifungal and antibacterial compounds, like taraxasterol and taraxanthin, can help combat fungal and bacterial infections that contribute to dandruff, reducing flaking and scaling on the scalp.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin is a type of fructan that helps soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and itching associated with dandruff.
Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol is a triterpenoid that has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, helping to control the growth of fungi that can contribute to dandruff and other scalp conditions.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment and reducing dandruff symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm the scalp.
Seeds: Used for their antifungal properties, which help control the growth of fungi causing dandruff.
Flowers: Used for their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help control the growth of microorganisms causing dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers at the peak of their potency and dry them completely for 2 hours.
Grind the dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 5g of the taraxacum officinale powder with 5g of a natural carrier agent in a ratio of 1:1.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 0.25g of the taraxacum officinale mixture each for 30 minutes.
Store the herbal taraxacum officinale capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass capsules helps with dandruff because of its unique combination of properties that soothe and calm the scalp. The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemongrass oil reduce redness and irritation, while its antifungal properties combat fungal overgrowth that can contribute to flaking skin. Additionally, lemongrass's natural antiseptic and antibacterial agents help to prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for the scalp to thrive, ultimately reducing dandruff symptoms and promoting a clear and flake-free complexion.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Lemongrass capsules have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal species, such as Malassezia, which is often associated with dandruff.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The active compounds in lemongrass capsules, like citral and geraniol, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce redness and irritation caused by dandruff.

Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass capsules contain antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to oxidative stress and scalp damage, both of which are thought to exacerbate dandruff.
remedy_constituents: Citral: A naturally occurring terpene that helps to reduce dandruff by inhibiting the growth of fungal pathogens, such as Malassezia, which contribute to scalp irritation and flaking.

Geraniol: A monoterpene alcohol that has antifungal and antimicrobial properties, which help to control the growth of fungi and bacteria on the scalp, thereby reducing dandruff and promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Limonene: A cyclic terpene with antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and flaking associated with dandruff.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Cymbopogon citratus are commonly used due to their high content of essential oils, particularly limonene and geraniol, which have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Cymbopogon citratus are used for their medicinal properties, including their ability to treat dandruff, as they contain compounds like geraniol and linalool with antifungal and soothing effects.
Stems: Stems of Cymbopogon citratus are also used, although less frequently, due to their content of essential oils, which can help in treating dandruff by reducing fungal growth and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves from a reputable source and store in an airtight container.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 1g of the ground cymbopogon citratus powder with 100g of gelatin in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes while stirring constantly with a spatula.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the cymbopogon citratus mixture using a capsule filler for 5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail capsules helps with dandruff because they contain silicates, which are essential minerals that help to normalize skin function and reduce flaking. The antioxidants present in the capsules also soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and itching associated with dandruff. Additionally, the anti-fungal properties of field horsetail help to prevent the growth of fungi that can contribute to dandruff, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment.
remedy_properties: Selenium content: Rich in selenium, horsetail helps to reduce inflammation and prevent oxidative stress, which can contribute to dandruff flaking.
Silica content: Horsetail's high silica content helps to strengthen hair follicles and promote healthy skin cells, reducing the likelihood of flaky, itchy scalp conditions like dandruff.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in horsetail capsules help to protect the scalp from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dandruff development.
remedy_constituents: Shikimic acid: Helps with dandruff by reducing inflammation and soothing the scalp, potentially alleviating irritation and flaking associated with dandruff.
Furanosesquiterpenes (e.g., arvenic acid): Have antifungal properties that can help control the growth of yeast-like fungi on the scalp, which can contribute to dandruff.
Quercetin: Acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation on the scalp, potentially alleviating symptoms of dandruff.
remedy_parts: Roots: Equisetum arvense roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which can help soothe and treat dandruff.
Stems: Equisetum arvense stems are used because they contain saponins, which can help to clean and purify the scalp, reducing dandruff.
Leaves: Equisetum arvense leaves are used for their anti-dandruff properties, which can help to reduce flaking and itching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh equisetum arvense stems in the early morning when the dew is still present on the plant.
Clean and dry the harvested stems thoroughly in a low-temperature oven at 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Crush the dried equisetum arvense stems into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to ensure uniformity.
Mix the powder with a binding agent such as gelatin at a 1:1 ratio by weight to facilitate encapsulation.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal powder mixture and seal them using a capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with dandruff because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the scalp. The capsules contain beta-carotene, an antioxidant that fights free radicals and reduces inflammation, helping to eliminate flakes and itchiness. Additionally, stinging nettle's antifungal and antibacterial properties combat fungal and bacterial overgrowth on the scalp, further contributing to a healthy, flake-free scalp and reduction in dandruff symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like beta-sitosterol, which may help reduce inflammation and itching associated with dandruff.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of stinging nettle capsules may help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and damage to the scalp, leading to conditions like dandruff.

Immunomodulatory effects: Stinging nettle capsules have been shown to modulate the immune system's response, which may help reduce the overactive inflammatory response associated with dandruff, allowing for a more balanced and healthy scalp.
remedy_constituents: Furanocoumarins: These compounds have been shown to possess antifungal properties, which can help control the growth of Malassezia, a fungus that contributes to dandruff.
Saponins: Saponins have been found to have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the scalp, reducing itching and flaking associated with dandruff.

Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have been reported to exhibit antifungal and antioxidant activities, which may help mitigate the symptoms of dandruff by reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm the scalp.
Roots: Contain ursolic acid, a compound that has been shown to have antifungal properties, helping to control fungal infections that can cause dandruff.
Stems: Contain a variety of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that can help alleviate dandruff symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems from a clean and pesticide-free area.
Dry the harvested leaves and stems in a single layer at 40°c for 2-3 hours.
Grind 1 teaspoon of dried urtica dioica into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground powder with a binding agent like gelatin or vegetable cellulose in a 1:1 ratio.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5-1 gram of the herbal mixture and seal them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with dandruff because of its remarkable anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The herb contains compounds like camphor, bornel, and thujone that help reduce scalp inflammation and itching caused by dandruff. Additionally, sage's natural antimicrobial agents combat fungal infections, which are a common underlying cause of dandruff. By addressing these underlying issues, sage capsules can effectively soothe and calm the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing flaking and redness associated with dandruff.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Sage contains compounds like thujone and camphor, which have antifungal effects that can help to reduce fungal infections that contribute to dandruff.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's flavonoids and phenolic acids possess anti-inflammatory activity, which can soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with dandruff.
Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidant compounds help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the scalp and exacerbate dandruff, promoting overall health and protection of the skin.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids in Salvia officinalis have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of fungi that contribute to dandruff.

Rosmarinic acid (a phenolic compound): Rosmarinic acid in Salvia officinalis has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and flaking associated with dandruff.

Ursolic acid (a triterpenoid): Ursolic acid in Salvia officinalis has antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help prevent the growth of fungi and bacteria that contribute to dandruff and other scalp irritations.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antifungal and antimicrobial properties, which help in treating dandruff and other scalp-related issues.
Seeds: They are used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in soothing and calming the scalp to reduce dandruff.
Roots: They are used due to their antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help in reducing the fungal infections that cause dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried salvia officinalis leaves from a reputable source.
Grind 2 tablespoons of the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar.
Mix 1/2 teaspoon of the powder with 1/2 teaspoon of cellulose powder in a small bowl.
Use a capsule machine to fill 20 empty capsules with the mixture.
Label and store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary capsules helps with dandruff because of its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oils present in rosemary have been shown to reduce fungal growth, which is a common cause of dandruff. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidants help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and irritation caused by flaking skin. As a result, rosemary capsules can help regulate scalp health and promote a healthy hair growth environment, ultimately leading to a reduction in dandruff symptoms and an improvement in overall scalp well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Rosemary's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress on the scalp, which can contribute to inflammation and flaking associated with dandruff.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Rosemary contains anti-inflammatory compounds like camphor and borneol, which may help reduce redness, itching, and swelling caused by dandruff.
Antimicrobial properties: Rosemary's antimicrobial activity can help control the growth of yeast-like fungi (Malassezia) that contribute to dandruff, reducing flaking and irritation.
remedy_constituents: Carvacrol: This monoterpene is known for its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the fungal growth and inflammation associated with dandruff.

Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can help to soothe the scalp, reduce flaking, and prevent the growth of pathogenic fungi that contribute to dandruff.

Rosmarinic acid: This polyphenolic compound has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the scalp, thus alleviating the symptoms of dandruff.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of antioxidants and essential oils, which help to soothe and calm the scalp.
Stems: The stems contain a high amount of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and irritation associated with dandruff.
Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high content of essential oils, which can help to calm and balance the scalp's pH, reducing the risk of dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves when they are in full bloom.
Dry the leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Use a coffee grinder to finely powder 20g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves.
Mix the powder with 10g of gelatin in a bowl to create a uniform mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the powder mixture and seal tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with dandruff because of its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The capsules contain a concentrated extract of lavender oil, which is known to soothe itchy scalps and reduce flaking caused by dandruff. Lavender's calming effects also help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of dandruff. As the capsules are easily absorbed into the bloodstream, they can target the root cause of dandruff from within, promoting a healthy scalp and reducing flakes and irritation for a smoother, more manageable hair experience.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Lavender's antifungal properties help to combat fungal infections that can contribute to dandruff, reducing flaking and itching.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties soothe itchy scalps and reduce redness and inflammation associated with dandruff, providing relief from discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidant properties help to protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dandruff symptoms and promote a healthy, balanced scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lavender roots help soothe and calm itchy scalps associated with dandruff.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids and vitamins, lavender seeds moisturize and nourish the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing flaking.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers, carefully drying them for 24 hours to preserve potency.
Grind dried lavender flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 250 milligrams of ground lavender powder with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture in a warm place for 48 hours, allowing the lavender extract to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth, discarding solids and transferring remaining liquid to empty capsules for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsules helps with dandruff because of its remarkable antifungal and antibacterial properties. The capsules contain thymol, a compound that inhibits the growth of fungi and bacteria that contribute to scalp irritation and flaking. Additionally, thyme's anti-inflammatory effects help reduce redness and itchiness associated with dandruff. As a result, regular use of herbal thyme capsules can soothe and calm the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth and reducing the appearance of flakes and flakes buildup.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Thyme's antifungal compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to combat fungal infections on the scalp, which can contribute to dandruff.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in thyme, including flavonoids and terpenes, reduce redness, swelling, and itchiness associated with dandruff, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to dandruff, reducing flaking and itching.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of thyme are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing scalp irritations and reducing inflammation associated with dandruff.
Barks: Thyme barks contain astringent and antibacterial properties that help to reduce the growth of yeast and fungi on the scalp, contributing to dandruff, while also calming irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and stems from your local garden or store-bought dried herbs in bulk.
Dry the harvested material using air circulation method for 7-10 days to ensure complete desiccation.
Crush dried thymus vulgaris into fine powder using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder to enhance bioavailability.
Mix 250mg of powdered thymus vulgaris with 500mg of gelatin capsules and fill them immediately to prevent contamination.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood capsules helps with dandruff because they contain the essential oils of Artemisia absinthium, which have natural antifungal and antibacterial properties. These properties help to reduce the growth of yeast on the scalp, a common cause of dandruff. Additionally, wormwood has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated skin and reduce redness. By reducing fungal and bacterial overgrowth, and calming inflammation, wormwood capsules can effectively treat dandruff, promoting a healthy and flake-free scalp.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood capsules contain sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling associated with dandruff.
Antifungal: The essential oils present in wormwood capsules, such as thujone and camphor, have antifungal properties that can help combat fungal infections that contribute to dandruff.
Antioxidant: Wormwood's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dandruff symptoms and slow down the healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of wormwood contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to soothe and treat dandruff.

Barks: Wormwood barks have been traditionally used to treat scalp conditions such as dandruff due to their antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help to reduce fungal infections and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried leaves of artemisia absinthium and grind into a fine powder.
Measure out one tablespoon of the ground herb and mix with two tablespoons of gelatin.
Add a small amount of distilled water to the mixture and stir until it forms a paste.
Use a capsule machine to fill empty capsules with the herbal paste and seal tightly.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container for up to six months and take twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Citronella capsules helps with dandruff because they contain natural oils that soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and irritation. The antimicrobial properties of citronella oil help to eliminate fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to dandruff. Additionally, citronella's antifungal and antibacterial properties help to control flaking and itching, promoting a healthy scalp environment and reducing the appearance of flakes. Regular use of citronella capsules may also help to reduce excessive oil production, further combating dandruff symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Citronella oil's antifungal properties help to combat fungal infections that can contribute to dandruff, such as Malassezia, by preventing their growth and spread.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in citronella oil reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with dandruff, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Antioxidant properties: Citronella's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage the scalp and contribute to dandruff, helping to protect the skin and promote healthy cell growth.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, rhyzomes help reduce inflammation and itching associated with dandruff.
Roots: Packed with antioxidants and antifungal properties, roots effectively combat fungal infections that contribute to dandruff.
Seeds: Containing essential oils, seeds provide a natural remedy for soothing scalp irritations and reducing flaking.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of dried cymbopogon nardus leaves from a trusted supplier or cultivate them in your garden.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for approximately 30 minutes.
Mix 10g of the powdered leaves with 90g of tapioca starch and 5g of gelatin in a bowl, then stir well for 5 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency, taking about 20 minutes per batch of 100 capsules.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature, away from direct sunlight, and use within 6 months to ensure maximum efficacy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum capsules helps with dandruff because they contain the natural anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties of Eucalyptus globulus. The capsules work to soothe itchy scalps, reduce flaking, and combat fungal infections that can contribute to dandruff. Additionally, the antioxidants present in the capsules help to protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth. By addressing these underlying issues, Tasmanian blue gum capsules provide relief from dandruff symptoms and promote a clear, flake-free scalp.
remedy_properties: Antifungal and antibacterial properties: These properties help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to dandruff, reducing flaking and scaling on the scalp.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Tasmanian Blue Gum capsules can help reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with dandruff, making it a natural soothing agent for irritated scalps.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of Tasmanian Blue Gum may help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and skin conditions like dandruff.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm irritated scalps.
Barks: Contain tannins that reduce inflammation and promote healing, making them effective against dandruff and flaking skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the eucalyptus leaves in a warm and dry place for at least 24 hours to prevent mold growth.
Crush the dried eucalyptus leaves into small particles using a mortar and pestle to enhance absorption.
Mix 1000mg of crushed eucalyptus leaves with 500mg of gelatin capsules and fill according to manufacturer's instructions in about 30 minutes.
Store the herbal eucalyptus globulus capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with dandruff because of their anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe an itchy scalp and reduce redness. The capsules also contain compounds that help to combat fungal infections, a common cause of dandruff. Additionally, ginger's natural antiseptic qualities help to kill bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to dandruff flaking. As a result, herbal ginger capsules can provide effective relief from the symptoms of dandruff, leaving your scalp feeling calm, clear, and healthy.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, can reduce inflammation in the scalp, which is a common underlying cause of dandruff.

Antimicrobial properties: Herbal ginger capsules contain antimicrobial compounds like gingerol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and yeast that contribute to dandruff and other scalp conditions.
Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidant properties can help protect the scalp from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dandruff and other skin issues.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, making them effective against scalp infections causing dandruff.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, barks help to soothe itching and inflammation, while also exhibiting antibacterial properties that combat fungal overgrowth on the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Grind dried zingiber officinale rhizomes into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powdered zingiber officinale with 50 milliliters of glycerin in a small bowl for 5 minutes.
Add 100 milliliters of distilled water to the mixture and stir well for 2 minutes.
Pour the liquid mixture into empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filler machine for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with dandruff because of their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herbs in these capsules, such as marshmallow root, work to calm irritated scalps and reduce redness and flaking. They also help to moisturize the scalp, leaving it feeling soft and nourished. Additionally, marshmallow capsules can help to reduce itching and irritation, allowing for a more comfortable scalp and hair growth.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: The mucilage property of marshmallow capsules helps to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and itching associated with dandruff.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation on the scalp caused by dandruff.
Emollients: The emollient properties of marshmallow capsules moisturize and nourish the scalp, helping to lock in moisture and reduce flaking associated with dry skin conditions like dandruff.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated scalp skin.
Buds: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and itching associated with dandruff.
Fruits: Rich in antioxidants, which protect the hair follicles and scalps from damage caused by free radicals.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis and cut it into small pieces.
Dry the cut marshmallow root pieces in a warm place for at least 24 hours.
Grind 50g of dried marshmallow root into a fine powder using an herb grinder.
Mix 1:5 ratio of marshmallow root powder with gelatin capsule shells and fill 100 empty capsules.
Label the filled capsules clearly stating the contents ingredients and dosage instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain capsules helps with dandruff because of their unique ability to soothe and calm the scalp. The natural anti-inflammatory properties of plantain help reduce redness and itching associated with dandruff, while its antifungal and antibacterial properties combat the underlying fungal and bacterial infections that cause flaking and scaling. Additionally, plantain's antioxidant properties help to strengthen the hair follicles and promote a healthy scalp environment, reducing the recurrence of dandruff and promoting a clear, healthy complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Plantain's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with dandruff, providing relief from scalp irritation.

Antifungal and antibacterial property: Plantain's antifungal and antibacterial properties combat fungal infections like Malassezia and bacteria that contribute to dandruff, promoting a healthy scalp environment.

Soothing and protective property: Plantain's mucilages and saponins create a soothing barrier on the scalp, protecting it from further irritation and inflammation while reducing flaking and scaling associated with dandruff.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in reducing redness and itching associated with dandruff.
Barks: The barks of the plantain plant contain compounds that help soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing flaking and inflammation caused by dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality plantago major leaves and flowers in the morning when dew is present.
Dry the plant material by spreading it thinly on paper bags or screens for 7-10 days.
Grind dried plantago major into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of powdered plantago major and seal immediately.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal capsules helps with dandruff because of its potent antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The herb's active compound, berberine, has been shown to inhibit the growth of malassezia, a yeast that contributes to scalp irritation and flaking. Additionally, goldenseal's anti-inflammatory effects help reduce redness and itching associated with dandruff, while its antibacterial properties combat fungal infections that can exacerbate the condition. By targeting these underlying causes, goldenseal capsules provide natural relief from dandruff symptoms and promote a healthy, flake-free scalp.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Has antibacterial and antifungal properties, which helps to eliminate fungal infections on the scalp that can contribute to dandruff.
Berberinoids: Have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce redness and swelling associated with dandruff, promoting healthy skin and reducing flaking.
Saponins: Have a foaming action that can help remove dead skin cells and other debris from the scalp, leaving it clean and flake-free.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Goldenseal are used to make medicinal capsules for dandruff as they contain berberine, a natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm the scalp.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Goldenseal are used due to their high content of berberine, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and combat fungal infections that can contribute to dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hydrastis canadensis roots in the autumn season ensuring proper identification.
Clean and dry the harvested roots by gently scrubbing off dirt and debris then air drying them for two weeks.
Grind 100g of dried hydrastis canadensis root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground root powder with 200ml of glycerin and 50ml of distilled water to create a liquid extract solution leaving it to steep for four hours.
Fill empty capsules with one-half teaspoon of the liquid extract solution then seal and store them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with dandruff because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. This helps to soothe an itchy scalp and reduce flaking, while also combating fungal infections that can contribute to dandruff. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to inhibit the growth of yeast and bacteria on the skin, further reducing symptoms of dandruff. By incorporating herbal licorice capsules into a scalp care routine, individuals may experience relief from itchy, flaky scalps and enjoy healthier, more balanced hair.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and flaking associated with dandruff.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice root have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-fungal activities, which can help combat fungal infections that may contribute to dandruff and reduce oxidative stress on the scalp.
Liquiritin: This compound has been found to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help inhibit the growth of yeast and other microorganisms that may exacerbate dandruff symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to soothe and calm scalp irritation associated with dandruff.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and itching caused by dandruff.
Barks: Harbors antibacterial and antifungal compounds that prevent infection and promote healthy scalp conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality glycyrrhiza glabra roots in a clean and dry environment.
Dry the harvested roots under shade or using a dehydrator for at least 24 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mill or coffee grinder for about 30 minutes.
Mix the powdered root with a binding agent such as water to form a uniform blend in approximately 15 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the herbal blend and seal them tightly for further use.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for dandruff are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the symptoms of dandruff by reducing inflammation, itching, and flaking of the scalp. These capsules typically contain a blend of herbal extracts, including Melaleuca alternifolia, Curcuma longa, Azadirachta indica, Aloe barbadensis, Cinnamomum camphora, Cymbopogon citratus, Zingiber officinale, Santalum album, Eucalyptus globulus, and Aloe vera. If left untreated, dandruff can lead to social anxiety, low self-esteem, and difficulty in maintaining personal hygiene, negatively impacting one's quality of life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dandruff that are treated with herbal capsules are psoriasis, eczema, and seborrheic dermatitis. These conditions are characterized by itching, redness, and flaking of the skin, and are often caused by fungal or yeast infections. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like turmeric, neem, and tea tree oil can help to soothe and calm the affected areas, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with dandruff is a blend of Neem, Amla, and Tea Tree Oil. Neem capsules help reduce fungal growth, while Amla capsules promote healthy scalp and hair growth. Tea Tree Oil capsules have antifungal and antibacterial properties that soothe and calm the scalp, reducing flaking and itchiness. This synergistic combination helps to heal dandruff, promote a healthy scalp, and prevent future breakouts, providing long-lasting relief from this common scalp condition. Regular use can lead to a healthy and dandruff-free scalp.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia capsules helps with dandruff because they contain tea tree oil, a natural antifungal agent that effectively targets the root cause of dandruff, a fungal infection. The oil's antiseptic properties help to reduce inflammation and soothe the scalp, preventing flaking and itching. Additionally, tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties help to control the growth of yeast and other microorganisms that contribute to dandruff, promoting a healthy scalp and reducing flaking. This natural remedy provides long-lasting relief from dandruff symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps with dandruff by reducing inflammation and microbial growth on the scalp, thereby alleviating symptoms of dandruff.
Terpinen-4-ol: Helps with dandruff by exhibiting antifungal and antibacterial properties, which prevent the growth of fungi and bacteria that contribute to dandruff.
Caryophyllene: Helps with dandruff by reducing inflammation and itching associated with dandruff, as well as by exhibiting antifungal properties that inhibit fungal growth on the scalp.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain oil with antifungal and antibacterial properties that help control dandruff-causing pathogens.
Barks: Rich in compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and heal scalp irritations.
Seeds: Contain oil with antimicrobial properties that help reduce flaking and itching associated with dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1000mg of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves from a reputable supplier and store them in an airtight container.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 250mg of the powdered leaves with 750mg of cellulose powder in a small bowl.
Fill 100 empty capsules with the leaf and cellulose mixture using a small spoon.
Label and date the filled capsules and store them in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa capsules helps with dandruff because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The curcumin present in these capsules reduces inflammation in the scalp, thereby preventing the flaking and itching associated with dandruff. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help to control the growth of fungi that can contribute to dandruff. As a result, Curcuma longa capsules promote a healthy scalp environment, reducing the occurrence of flakes and promoting a balanced scalp ecosystem. This leads to a smoother, healthier-looking scalp.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps reduce inflammation in the scalp, thereby alleviating symptoms of dandruff.
Demethoxycurcumin: It has potent antioxidant properties that help protect the scalp from oxidative stress, which can contribute to dandruff.
Volatile oil: It has antimicrobial properties that help control the growth of fungi and bacteria on the scalp, which can cause dandruff.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa contain a high concentration of essential oils, including turmerone, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to control dandruff-causing fungi and bacteria.
Seeds: Turmeric seeds are sometimes used to make capsules, as they contain a high amount of curcumin and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy scalp function.
remedy_recipe: Extract dried curcuma longa root powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the extracted curcuma longa root powder with 3 tablespoons of gelatin and 3 tablespoons of water.
Heat the gelatin mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes until it dissolves completely.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the heated curcuma longa root mixture using a spoon or dropper.
Seal the filled capsules using a capsule sealer for 5 minutes to ensure they are properly closed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Azadirachta indica capsules helps with dandruff because they contain the extracts of Neem leaves, which are rich in antifungal and antibacterial properties. These properties help in controlling the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause dandruff, thereby reducing flaking and itching of the scalp. The anti-inflammatory properties of Neem also soothe the scalp, reducing redness and irritation associated with dandruff. Regular use of Azadirachta indica capsules can help in maintaining a healthy scalp and preventing the recurrence of dandruff.
remedy_constituents: Azadirachtin: It helps with dandruff by inhibiting the growth of fungal and bacterial pathogens that contribute to scalp irritation and inflammation.
Phenolic acids: These compounds help alleviate dandruff by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce oxidative stress and soothe the scalp.

Sesquiterpenes: Sesquiterpenes in Azadirachta indica capsules help with dandruff by displaying antifungal and antibacterial activities, thus preventing the overgrowth of microorganisms that cause scalp irritation and dandruff.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their high content of neem oil, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help in treating dandruff.
Leaves: They are used due to their high content of azadirachtin, which has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help in soothing and treating dandruff.
Seeds (again, due to its high neem oil content, which is a key ingredient in treating dandruff):
remedy_recipe: Gather 500 grams of dried azadirachta indica leaves and flowers from a trusted supplier.
Grind the dried leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 10 minutes.
Mix 200 grams of the powder with 100 grams of vegetable cellulose capsules in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Fill 1000 capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filling machine for 15 minutes.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place for 24 hours before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis capsules helps with dandruff because of their anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The gel extracted from Aloe vera helps soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and redness associated with dandruff. Aloe vera also has antioxidant properties that promote healthy hair growth and improve scalp health. By regulating the pH balance of the scalp and reducing the production of excess oil, Aloe barbadensis capsules help control dandruff, leaving the scalp feeling clean, refreshed, and healthy. Regular use can lead to a reduction in flakes and a healthier scalp.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: This anthraquinone compound helps to reduce scaling and flaking by promoting the shedding of dead skin cells, thereby reducing dandruff.

Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone compound has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and fungal infections that can contribute to dandruff.
Cinnamic acid: This phenolic compound has antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce the growth of fungi and bacteria that can cause dandruff and other scalp irritations.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used because they contain the highest concentration of aloe vera's active ingredients, including aloin and aloe-emodin, which help to soothe and treat scalp irritations.
Gel from Leaves: They are used because the gel obtained from the leaves is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene, which helps to nourish and moisturize the scalp, reducing dandruff.

Extract from Leaves: They are used because the extract from the leaves contains anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which help to reduce inflammation, itching, and fungal growth on the scalp associated with dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5-7 aloe barbadensis leaves from a trusted source and cut them into small pieces.
Extract the gel from the aloe pieces using a juicer or blender for 10 minutes.
Mix 1/4 teaspoon of vitamin e oil with 10 grams of the aloe extract in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the aloe mixture and seal them immediately.
Store the capsules in a cool dark place for up to 12 months or as directed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum camphora capsules helps with dandruff because of its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules contain oils extracted from the Camphor tree, which have been traditionally used to treat scalp conditions, including dandruff. The antifungal properties of Cinnamomum camphora capsules help to control the growth of yeast on the scalp, while its anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the scalp, reducing flaking and itching associated with dandruff. This makes it an effective natural remedy for managing dandruff symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This constituent has antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help in reducing the growth of fungi and bacteria that contribute to dandruff.
Limonene: As a natural antifungal agent, limonene helps to control the fungal infections that cause dandruff, promoting a healthy scalp.
Caryophyllene oxide: This constituent has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and itchiness associated with dandruff.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used due to their high camphor content, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help control dandruff.
Barks: Barks are often used to create capsules for dandruff treatment due to their ability to reduce inflammation and kill fungi that cause dandruff.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are utilized to make dandruff capsules because they contain camphor and other compounds that help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing dandruff symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried cinnamomum camphora bark at the right time to ensure optimal potency and flavor.
Grind 200g of the harvested cinnamomum camphora bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 10g of the ground cinnamomum camphora powder with 1g of magnesium stearate in a mortar and pestle.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 0.2g of the cinnamomum camphora mixture each using a powder filling machine.
Label and seal the 500 filled capsules in a clean environment to prevent contamination and ensure authenticity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus capsules helps with dandruff because they are rich in essential oils that possess antifungal and antibacterial properties. The lemongrass oil in these capsules helps to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and inflammation that can lead to dandruff. Additionally, the oil's antiseptic properties help to eliminate fungal and bacterial growth that can contribute to dandruff. This results in a healthier scalp and a reduction in the appearance of flakes, promoting overall scalp well-being. Regular use can lead to long-term dandruff management.
remedy_constituents: Citral: This terpene helps reduce dandruff by its antifungal properties, inhibiting the growth of Malassezia, a yeast that contributes to scalp irritation and flaking.
Geraniol: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing redness and irritation associated with dandruff.
Limonene: This terpene has antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help eliminate fungal infections on the scalp, thereby preventing flaking and irritation caused by dandruff.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of essential oils, particularly limonene and geraniol, which have antifungal and antimicrobial properties that help control dandruff.
Rhyzomes: Utilized for their oil content, which includes compounds like geraniol and citral that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, helping to soothe and treat dandruff.
Stems: Employed for their ability to provide a sustained release of essential oils, which help to control fungal growth and reduce dandruff symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves from mature plants.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer for 7-10 days at 30-40 degrees celsius.
Grind 15 grams of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix the powder with 15 grams of gelatin and 10 grams of water to create a paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500-700 milligrams of the paste and seal tightly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with dandruff because they contain ginger, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that soothes and calms the scalp. The antifungal properties of ginger help to combat fungal infections that can cause dandruff, reducing flaking and itchiness. Additionally, ginger's ability to improve blood circulation may help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin cell growth, leading to a reduction in dandruff symptoms and a healthier scalp overall. Regular use of these capsules may help to alleviate dandruff and promote a balanced scalp ecosystem.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and itching associated with dandruff.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols possess anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which can help control fungal growth and soothe scalp irritation.
Curcuminoids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the scalp from damage, reduce inflammation, and promote healthy skin cell growth.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used because they contain the highest concentration of active compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help combat dandruff.

Roots: Roots are used because they have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Chinese medicine to treat various scalp issues, including dandruff, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Buds: Buds are used because they contain high levels of essential oils, which have antifungal and antibacterial properties that help control the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried zingiber officinale rhizomes from a trusted supplier or cultivate it yourself in ideal conditions for 6 months.
Grind the rhizomes into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powder with a binding agent such as gelatin or vegetable cellulose in a 1:3 ratio by weight.
Fill empty capsule shells with the powder mixture using a powder filler or scoop for 10 minutes.
Seal the capsules immediately after filling to preserve the potency and shelf life for 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Santalum album capsules helps with dandruff because they possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively combat the fungal infection causing the condition. The antifungal properties of the capsules help in controlling the growth of Malassezia, a fungus that contributes to dandruff. Additionally, the capsules' ability to reduce inflammation and soothe the scalp helps in calming irritation and flaking, promoting a healthy scalp and preventing the recurrence of dandruff. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective solution to treat dandruff.
remedy_constituents: Santalol: Helps with dandruff by its antimicrobial properties, reducing the growth of fungi and bacteria that contribute to scalp infections and flaking.
α-Santalol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and irritation on the scalp associated with dandruff, and soothing the skin to promote a healthy scalp environment.
α-Terpineol: Possesses antifungal properties, which help control the growth of yeast and fungi on the scalp that can lead to dandruff, itchiness, and flaking.
remedy_parts: Essential oil from the Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Santalum album due to their high concentration of essential oils, which are used to treat dandruff.
Leaves: The leaves of Santalum album are also used to create capsules for dandruff treatment due to their antiseptic properties that help in soothing and calming the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Dry 100 grams of santalum album bark in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried santalum album bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 10 grams of the santalum album powder with 10 grams of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture from step 3 using a capsule filling machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus capsules helps with dandruff because of its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The capsules contain extracts from the leaves of the eucalyptus tree, which have been traditionally used to soothe and calm the scalp. The eucalyptus oil present in the capsules helps to reduce irritation, itchiness, and flaking associated with dandruff. By reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy scalp environment, Eucalyptus globulus capsules can help to alleviate dandruff symptoms, promoting a smooth and healthy-looking scalp.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps with dandruff by reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Catechin: Contributes to dandruff relief by displaying antimicrobial properties that combat fungal infections causing the condition.
Pinene: Aids in dandruff treatment by reducing fungal growth and promoting a balanced scalp pH.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Eucalyptus globulus are the primary source of eucalyptol, a key ingredient in dandruff treatments due to its antifungal and antiseptic properties.
Barks: Eucalyptus bark is rich in compounds with antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm the scalp to treat dandruff.

Essential oil extracted from Leaves: Eucalyptus essential oil, extracted from the leaves, is commonly used in dandruff treatments due to its decongestant, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties that help alleviate symptoms of dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves to ensure quality and freshness of the herb.
Dry the harvested leaves at 50°c for 2 hours to remove moisture and preserve potency.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to facilitate extraction.
Mix 1g of the ground powder with 1g of vegetable cellulose in a small bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the powder blend using an encapsulation machine to produce 120 capsules.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera capsules helps with dandruff because of its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant is rich in vitamins, minerals, and fatty acids that soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and flaking. By regulating the pH balance of the scalp and promoting healthy hair growth, aloe vera capsules can help to alleviate dandruff symptoms, leaving the scalp feeling refreshed and healthy. This natural remedy can also help to prevent future dandruff occurrences.
remedy_constituents: Vitamins A, C, and E: These antioxidants help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and irritation that can lead to dandruff.
Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and itching associated with dandruff, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Glucomannans: These prebiotic fibers help to balance the scalp's natural flora, preventing the overgrowth of yeast and bacteria that can contribute to dandruff.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain the highest concentration of aloe vera gel, which is rich in anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help soothe and treat dandruff.
Seeds: They are rich in nutrients such as vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy scalp conditions.
Roots: They contain a compound called aloin, which has antifungal properties that help to control dandruff-causing fungi and promote a healthy scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 aloe vera leaves with thick fleshy gel inside and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Peel off the outer green skin from the leaves to expose the clear gel inside and then chop into small pieces.
Mix 1 tablespoon of chopped aloe vera gel with 1 tablespoon of gelling agent such as carrageenan powder and 1 tablespoon of glycerin in a bowl.
Add 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil and 1 teaspoon of lemon juice to the mixture and stir well to combine.
Fill a capsule machine with the mixture and produce 100-200 herbal aloe vera capsules in about 10-15 minutes.
